text: Highlight Clip Arthur Hayes: 99% of Altcoins Going to Zero Does Not Mean the End of the Industry On May 5, 2026, during a CoinDesk interview at Consensus, BitMEX co-founder Arthur Hayes stated that altcoins will never die despite institutional adoption. He believes that while 99% of altcoins will go to zero, this mirrors the stock market: over a long period since 1929, about 98% of S&P 500 companies have also gone to zero, making most stocks shitcoins as well. He expressed that 24/7 crypto trading merely compresses this cycle, and it remains a good model for raising capital and innovation. — link

text: Payward Applies for OCC Trust Charter to Expand Kraken's Regulated Crypto Custody Business Payward, the parent company of Kraken, has applied for a national trust company charter with the OCC to establish Payward National Trust Company (PNTC), a federally regulated entity focused on digital asset custody services for institutional clients. text: Foreign Central Banks Boost Share of Malaysian Bonds to Record • Foreign central banks now own a record 36% of Malaysia's sovereign bonds, from 29.4% a year ago. text: Aptos Foundation and Aptos Labs announced a commitment of over $50 million across first-party products, research, protocol infrastructure, and a strategic fund for trading and AI partners. Aptos said Decibel is live on mainnet with over $1 billion in cumulative volume. Stablecoin market cap on Aptos has reached $1.93 billion, while RWAs have reached $1.2 billion, with BlackRock, Franklin Templeton, Apollo Global and other asset managers deployed on the network. Aptos also plans to advance encrypted mempool, FIX and CCXT connectivity, multi-leader consensus, and confidential perpetual trading. — link

text: Highlight Clip Tom Lee: Ethereum Is the Best-Performing Asset Since the War Started BitMine Chairman Tom Lee said in a May 5 speech at Paris Blockchain Week 2026 that Ethereum has been the world's best-performing asset since the Middle East crisis began, even outperforming energy stocks and beating the S&P 500 by nearly 20 percentage points. He said that if markets can weather the Middle East crisis and the U.S. economy remains resilient despite higher oil prices, the crypto bull market could last into 2028. Lee also said Ethereum is going through a major deep consolidation, with tokenization and agentic AI likely to become key drivers of the next potential rally, including stablecoins, tokenized stocks, tokenized reputation systems and AI payment demand. — link
